events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: belt mistracking causes (loading off-center vs. idler skew - FD). This is a conveyor system hazard. Belt mistracking can cause spillage, belt damage, entanglement hazards. Need to identify hazards, severity, controls.

Belt mistracking due to loading off-center or idler skew. This is a defect in installation/alignment. Could lead to belt rubbing against structure, spillage, potential for belt fire, entanglement. OSHA 1910.212 covers machine guarding. CEMA covers conveyor safety.

Decision: fail because it's a defective/non-compliant condition that creates hazards while work is in progress.

Risk: high (mistracking can cause serious hazards).

Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.

Percent complete: maybe 70% since in-progress.

Findings: belt mistracking, idler skew, off-center loading, inadequate guarding.

Actions: stop conveyor, lock out/tag out, correct idler alignment, adjust loading chute, verify tracking, inspect guarding.

References: CEMA, OSHA 1910.212.

"findings": [
"Belt mistracking observed during operation, indicating idler skew or misalignment",
"Loading is off-center, contributing to lateral belt drift and uneven material distribution",
"Mistracking creates risk of belt contact with structure, spillage, and potential entanglement at nip points",
"Machine guarding under OSHA 1910.212 not effectively verified while conveyor is running with a tracking defect"
],
"actions": [
"Stop the conveyor and lock out/tag out before any adjustment",
"Inspect and correct idler alignment and skew per CEMA installation tolerances",
"Re-center or adjust the loading chute/feed point to ensure material loads on belt centerline",
"Verify belt tracking under no-load and loaded conditions after correction",
"Confirm all nip points, pinch points, and transfer areas are guarded per OSHA 1910.212 before return to service"
],
"rationale": "Belt mistracking from idler skew and off-center loading is an installed-defective and non-compliant condition that creates spillage, entanglement, and structural-contact hazards. The conveyor must be stopped, locked out, corrected, and re-verified before continued operation.",
"references": ["CEMA", "OSHA 1910.212"]
}
